summary.intent: Allow channel-bound sessions (Discord, Telegram, etc.) to be renamed by users, fixing GitHub issue #523.
summary.outcome: Backend and frontend guards blocking rename were removed and an E2E test was added, but the commit failed repeatedly due to GPG/pinentry TTY issues in the Claude environment.

repo: Channel routing uses session key (channel_type:account_id:chat_id) and channel_binding JSON blob — never the display label. Renaming is safe.
default_channel_session_key() at channel_events.rs:26 constructs deterministic session keys from channel_type, account_id, chat_id.
list_channel_sessions() and resolve_channel_session() route messages via the channel_binding column, not the label.
The frontend isChannel flag is computed from both key prefix (telegram:, msteams:) and channelBinding presence for Discord.
The GraphQL patch mutation shares the same backend path as the RPC, so iOS/macOS apps benefit from backend changes automatically.

code.finding: Guard that rejected label changes when channel_binding is present — removed to allow renaming.
canRename condition excluded isChannel — removed isChannel from the condition while keeping the variable since it's used elsewhere.
default_channel_session_key() builds deterministic routing keys from channel_type:account_id:chat_id.

workflow: Beads DB (issue tracker) was intermittently unavailable — work proceeded without creating the tracking issue.
GPG commit signing with pinentry-curses fails inside Claude's environment because it cannot open a TTY; pinentry-mac would be required.
Checking for stale error strings in tests after removing error-producing code is a useful verification step.

friction: Beads DB was down, preventing issue creation at the start of the session.
GPG signing failed repeatedly (pinentry-curses cannot open a TTY in the Claude environment); commit was never completed.
No pinentry-mac configured in ~/.gnupg/gpg-agent.conf, causing fallback to curses which is incompatible with this environment.
open_items: Commit, push, and PR creation are unfinished — user needs to either configure pinentry-mac or commit manually from terminal.
Beads issue was never created due to DB downtime — needs to be filed when DB recovers.
Improving default channel session names (e.g. 'Discord #general' instead of 'Discord 1') was explicitly deferred as a follow-up issue requiring channel event flow changes per provider.
